tree_manager_profiles.name: Emily Johnson
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Family historian focusing on Scandinavian and Midwestern US ancestry. Managing several interconnected public trees.

tree_manager_profiles.bio: Researching Taylor lines from Virginia, Kentucky, and Illinois with DNA-verified branches.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Maria Gonzalez
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Specializes in Mexican civil registration and Catholic parish records. Maintains multiple public trees for Gonzalez and Diaz families.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Sophia Kowalska
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Polish researcher with expertise in Galicia and Prussian partition records.

tree_manager_profiles.name: David Chen
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Documenting Chinese diaspora families in North America and Southeast Asia.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Linda Smith-Taylor
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Coordinating a large Taylor-Smith one-name study and public collaborative tree.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Ahmed Hassan
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Tracing extended Hassan and Ali families across North Africa and Europe.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Olivia Brown
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Focus on African American genealogy and Freedmen's Bureau records.

tree_manager_profiles.name: Peter Taylor
tree_manager_profiles.bio: Managing an extended Taylor family mega-tree that merges several Midwestern branches.

public_trees.name: Midwestern Taylor Mega-Tree
public_trees.description: An extensive public tree connecting multiple Taylor branches across Illinois, Wisconsin, and Kentucky, with DNA-verified lines and sourced records.

public_trees.name: Taylor-Smith One-Name Study Tree
public_trees.description: Collaborative one-name study for all Taylor and Smith-Taylor lines in North America and the British Isles.

public_trees.name: Virginia & Kentucky Taylor Lines
public_trees.description: Focuses on early Taylor families in colonial Virginia and their descendants who migrated into Kentucky and Illinois.

public_trees.name: Chicago Taylor Descendants Tree
public_trees.description: Tracks descendants of a 19th-century Taylor family who settled on Chicago's South Side.

public_trees.name: Johnson Families of Scandinavia & the Midwest
public_trees.description: Public tree tracing Johnson families from Sweden and Norway into Minnesota, Wisconsin, and the Dakotas.

public_trees.name: Gonzalez & Diaz Families of Northern Mexico
public_trees.description: Interconnected Gonzalez and Diaz lines from Nuevo León, Coahuila, and Texas border communities.

public_trees.name: Central Mexico Diaz Tree
public_trees.description: Diaz families from Mexico City, Puebla, and surrounding regions, linked through parish and civil registrations.

public_trees.name: O'Connor Clans of County Kerry
public_trees.description: Irish O'Connor families centered in County Kerry with branches in the US and Australia.

public_trees.name: Kowalski Families of Galicia & Prussian Poland
public_trees.description: Kowalski lines reconstructed from parish registers and civil records across former Galicia and Prussian territories.

public_trees.name: Master Tree of Polish Partition-Era Families
public_trees.description: A wide-scope project including many Polish surnames, with Kowalski as the anchor surname.

public_trees.name: Chen & Allied Families of the Chinese Diaspora
public_trees.description: Documents Chen families who migrated from Guangdong and Fujian to North America and Southeast Asia.

public_trees.name: Hassan Families of North Africa & Europe
public_trees.description: A public tree tracking Hassan and related surnames from Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia into European cities.

public_trees.name: Brown African American Heritage Tree
public_trees.description: Reconstructs Brown family lines from the US South after emancipation using Freedmen's Bureau and census records.

public_trees.name: Taylor-Brown Intersection Tree
public_trees.description: Smaller public tree focused on households where the Taylor and Brown families intermarried in Illinois and Missouri.

public_trees.name: Private Taylor Test Branch
public_trees.description: A small experimental Taylor branch kept private for living-relative privacy.

branch_groups.name: Midwest Taylor Heritage Branch
branch_groups.description: Branch group for Taylor descendants with roots in Illinois, Wisconsin, Michigan, and neighboring Midwestern states. Focus on sharing records, photos, and reunion planning.

branch_groups.name: Chicago South Side Taylor Branch
branch_groups.description: Taylor families who lived on Chicago’s South Side in the late 19th and 20th centuries. Connected to several public Taylor trees.

branch_groups.name: Global Taylor DNA & Records Project
branch_groups.description: Worldwide Taylor surname project combining autosomal and Y-DNA matches with document-based research.

branch_groups.name: Taylor Families of England & Scotland
branch_groups.description: Branch group exploring Taylor lines originating in England and Scotland before migrating abroad.

branch_groups.name: Taylor Cousins Circle (Private)
branch_groups.description: Private coordination space for a small circle of living Taylor cousins sharing living-relative details.

branch_groups.name: Gonzalez Families of Northern Mexico
branch_groups.description: Gonzalez cousins centered in Nuevo León, Coahuila, and Tamaulipas, sharing civil and parish records.

branch_groups.name: Central Mexico Diaz Branch
branch_groups.description: Diaz family researchers collaborating on lines from Mexico City and Puebla.

branch_groups.name: Kowalski Roots in Galicia
branch_groups.description: For descendants of Kowalski families from the historical region of Galicia (now Poland and Ukraine).

branch_groups.name: Scandinavian Johnsons of the Upper Midwest
branch_groups.description: Johnson families with Swedish and Norwegian origins who settled in Minnesota, Wisconsin, and the Dakotas.

branch_groups.name: Brown African American Heritage Branch
branch_groups.description: Descendants of Brown families from the US South, focusing on Reconstruction-era and Freedmen’s Bureau records.

branch_groups.name: Chen Diaspora Research Network
branch_groups.description: Chen surname researchers tracing migration from Guangdong and Fujian to North America and Southeast Asia.

branch_groups.name: Hassan Families of North Africa & Europe
branch_groups.description: Group for Hassan descendants whose families moved from North Africa into European cities in the 20th century.

branch_groups.name: County Kerry O’Connor Clans
branch_groups.description: O’Connor cousins descended from County Kerry families, coordinating Irish research trips and DNA testing.

forum_topics.title: Looking for information on John Taylor born 1850 in England
forum_topics.body:
Hi everyone,

I am researching my ancestor John Taylor, who was born around 1850 in England, possibly in the Manchester area. I am looking for suggestions on which record sets to check (civil registrations, parish records, census, etc.) and tips for dealing with such a common surname.

If anyone has experience tracing Taylor families in 19th-century England or knows good online resources, I would really appreciate your guidance.

Thank you!

forum_topics.title: How do I start my first family tree on this site?
forum_topics.body:
I just signed up and I am not sure how to begin building my family tree. Is there a step-by-step guide or wizard that walks through adding myself, my parents, and grandparents?

Also, can I edit relationships later if I make a mistake?

forum_topics.title: New advanced search filters for historical records
forum_topics.body:
We have rolled out new advanced search filters for historical records, including birth year ranges, exact or broad birthplace matching, and sortable results by birth year or relevance. You can access these features from the "Search Records" page by clicking the "Advanced Search" button.

Please try them out and share feedback in the Site Help category.

forum_topics.title: What is your favorite genealogy research trick?
forum_topics.body:
I thought it would be fun and useful to share everyones favorite genealogy tip or "trick" that helped break down a brick wall. It could be anything from using city directories in a creative way to tracking down collateral relatives.

What small technique made a big difference in your research?

forum_posts.body:
Hi everyone,

I am researching my ancestor John Taylor, who was born around 1850 in England, possibly in the Manchester area. I am looking for suggestions on which record sets to check (civil registrations, parish records, census, etc.) and tips for dealing with such a common surname.

If anyone has experience tracing Taylor families in 19th-century England or knows good online resources, I would really appreciate your guidance.

Thank you!

For a John Taylor born around 1850 in Manchester, start with the England & Wales Civil Registration Birth Index and search 1849–1851 in the Manchester registration districts. Once you have a likely certificate, use the parents names to locate him in the 1851 and 1861 censuses.

Because Taylor is so common, always correlate address and occupation information across records. Manchester parish registers (especially for the Church of England) are also very helpful and many are available online through major genealogy sites.

In addition to civil and parish records, dont forget to check city directories for Manchester in the 1870s–1890s. They can help you distinguish between multiple men named John Taylor by address and occupation.

If you know of any relatives who emigrated, passenger lists can also provide clues about the correct family group.

I just signed up and I am not sure how to begin building my family tree. Is there a step-by-step guide or wizard that walks through adding myself, my parents, and grandparents?

Also, can I edit relationships later if I make a mistake?

Yes, there is a guided wizard. From the Home page, click "Start Your Family Tree" and it will walk you through adding yourself, your parents, and your four grandparents including birth years.

You can always edit relationships later by opening a person in the tree view and using the "Edit Relationships" option, so dont worry if you make a mistake at first.

We have rolled out new advanced search filters for historical records, including birth year ranges, exact or broad birthplace matching, and sortable results by birth year or relevance. You can access these features from the "Search Records" page by clicking the "Advanced Search" button.

Please try them out and share feedback in the Site Help category.

I thought it would be fun and useful to share everyones favorite genealogy tip or "trick" that helped break down a brick wall. It could be anything from using city directories in a creative way to tracking down collateral relatives.

What small technique made a big difference in your research?

My favorite trick is to map out all neighbors from census pages and city directories for two or three decades. Collateral families and neighbors often turn up as in-laws later, and that context helped me identify the correct line more than once.
